message.content:
Validation error: Unrecognized key(s) in object: 'tools', 'finishing_touches' in .coderabbit/yaml

coderabbit.yaml has unrecognized properties
CodeRabbit is using all valid settings from your configuration. Unrecognized properties (listed below) have been ignored and may indicate typos or deprecated fields that can be removed.

⚠️ Parsing warnings (1)
Validation error: Unrecognized key(s) in object: 'tools', 'finishing_touches'
⚙️ Configuration instructions
Please see the configuration documentation for more information.
You can also validate your configuration using the online YAML validator.
If your editor has YAML language server enabled, you can add the path at the top of this file to enable auto-completion and validation: # yaml-language-server: $schema=https://coderabbit.ai/integrations/schema.v2.json
📝

attachment.content:
- update-config: Use this skill to configure the Claude Code harness via settings.json. Automated behaviors ("from now on when X", "each time X", "whenever X", "before/after X") require hooks configured in settings.json - the harness executes these, not Claude, so memory/preferences cannot fulfill them. Also use for: permissions ("allow X", "add permission", "move permission to"), env vars ("set X=Y"), hook troubleshooting, or any changes to settings.json/settings.local.json files. Examples: "allow npm commands", "add bq permission to global settings", "move permission to user settings", "set DEBUG=true", "when claude stops show X". For simple settings like theme/model, suggest the /config command.
- keybindings-help: Use when the user wants to customize keyboard shortcuts, rebind keys, add chord bindings, or modify ~/.claude/keybindings.json. Examples: "rebind ctrl+s", "add a chord shortcut", "change the submit key", "customize keybindings".
- simplify: Review changed code for reuse, quality, and efficiency, then fix any issues found.
- fewer-permission-prompts: Scan your transcripts for common read-only Bash and MCP tool calls, then add a prioritized allowlist to project .claude/settings.json to reduce permission prompts.
- loop: Run a prompt or slash command on a recurring interval (e.g. /loop 5m /foo). Omit the interval to let the model self-pace. - When the user wants to set up a recurring task, poll for status, or run something repeatedly on an interval (e.g. "check the deploy every 5 minutes", "keep running /babysit-prs"). Do NOT invoke for one-off tasks.
- schedule: Create, update, list, or run scheduled remote agents (routines) on a cron schedule or once at a specific time. - When the user wants to schedule a recurring remote agent, set up automated tasks, create a cron job for Claude Code, or manage their scheduled agents/routines. Also use when the user wants a one-time scheduled run ("run this once at 3pm", "remind me to check X tomorrow").
- claude-api: Build, debug, and optimize Claude API / Anthropic SDK apps. Apps built with this skill should include prompt caching. Also handles migrating existing Claude API code between Claude model versions (4.5 → 4.6, 4.6 → 4.7, retired-model replacements).
TRIGGER when: code imports `anthropic`/`@anthropic-ai/sdk`; user asks for the Claude API, Anthropic SDK, or Managed Agents; user adds/modifies/tunes a Claude feature (caching, thinking, compaction, tool use, batch, files, citations, memory) or model (Opus/Sonnet/Haiku) in a file; questions about prompt caching / cache hit rate in an Anthropic SDK project.
SKIP: file imports `openai`/other-provider SDK, filename like `*-openai.py`/`*-generic.py`, provider-neutral code, general programming/ML.
- foundation-creator: Use this skill when the user wants to write, draft, update, or revise a top-level foundation document for a company, product, or new primitive. This document captures thesis, mission, boundaries, actor model, surfaces, strategic bets, and open questions without collapsing ambiguity into implementation decisions. Applies when the user is still defining what the system is, what it is not, and what long-term direction it implies. Does NOT apply to concrete service specifications in `SPEC.md`, implementation plans, PRDs, or roadmap execution docs.
- find-skills: Helps users discover and install agent skills when they ask questions like "how do I do X", "find a skill for X", "is there a skill that can...", or express interest in extending capabilities. This skill should be used when the user is looking for functionality that might exist as an installable skill.
- spec-creator: Use this skill when the user wants to write, draft, update, or revise a top-level SPEC.md service specification — the language-agnostic behavioral spec that sits at a project's root and describes what a long-running service or automation does (problem statement, goals/non-goals, components, domain model). Applies when the user says things like "write a spec for X", "draft a specification", "update SPEC.md to add Y", "revise the non-goals", "add a new component to the spec", or hands over notes and asks for them to be turned into a formal service spec. Also applies when the user references an existing SPEC.md and wants edits, even if they don't explicitly use the word "spec". Covers both greenfield creation and targeted updates of an existing file. Does NOT apply to per-topic specs under thoughts/shared/specs/ (those are produced by create_ralph_topics from a JTBD), nor to JTBDs, implementation plans, or PRDs.
- lightfast-aeo: Use when authoring or reviewing any Lightfast content destined for AI engine retrieval —
release notes, blog posts, docs pages, integration pages, anything published to the
public site. Triggers on requests to write, draft, rewrite, or audit prose that should
be cited by ChatGPT, Perplexity, Codex, Google AI Overviews, or other answer engines,
even when the user doesn't say "AEO", "citations", or "answer engine". Per-content-type
skills compose this for voice and section ordering — load this one too whenever they
activate.
- lightfast-changelog: Write and review AEO-optimized changelog entries for Lightfast. Triggers when the user asks
to write a changelog entry, review changelog content, or asks about changelog structure,
AEO optimization, or release notes formatting.
- lightfast-sdk: Use Lightfast tools to search across team knowledge and interact with connected providers
(GitHub, Linear, Sentry, Vercel, Apollo). Triggers when the user asks about errors, issues,
PRs, deployments, or wants to search across their dev tools. Also triggers proactively when
investigating bugs, reviewing code, or checking deployment status.
- lightfast-debug: General-purpose Lightfast debugging workflow. Triggers when the user asks to debug,
investigate, trace, reproduce, inspect, or diagnose behavior across the app, platform,
Inngest workflows, database state, connected providers, or observability tooling. Use as
the broad entrypoint when no narrower skill is clearly better, when the failing surface is
unclear, or when a bug may cross multiple systems. Prefer `lightfast-db`,
`lightfast-inngest`, or `lightfast-sdk` for direct single-surface requests that clearly map
to one of those tools.
- lightfast-inngest: Inspect Inngest function runs, debug failures, send events, and invoke functions via the
local Inngest dev server MCP. Triggers when the user asks about background jobs, workflow
runs, failed steps, event delivery, or wants to invoke/test Inngest functions.
- lightfast-clerk: Provision Clerk test users, sign in via the browser, mint JWTs, and tear
everything down — for local-dev tRPC / desktop / API testing. Triggers when
the user wants to call Lightfast tRPC procedures with a real auth token,
test desktop sign-in flows, or set up / clean up test users in Clerk dev.
Dev-only: refuses to run against pk_live_ keys or non-localhost URLs.
- lightfast-db: Query and inspect the Postgres database schema and data. Triggers when the user asks about
tables, columns, rows, database schema, wants to run a SELECT query, or needs to understand
the data model. Read-only — cannot modify data.
- turborepo: Turborepo monorepo build system guidance. Triggers on: turbo.json, task pipelines,
dependsOn, caching, remote cache, the "turbo" CLI, --filter, --affected, CI optimization, environment
variables, internal packages, monorepo structure/best practices, and boundaries.

Use when user: configures tasks/workflows/pipelines, creates packages, sets up
monorepo, shares code between apps, runs changed/affected packages, debugs cache,
or has apps/packages directories.
- implement_plan: Implement technical plans from thoughts/shared/plans with verification
- create_plan: Create detailed implementation plans through interactive research and iteration
- validate_plan: Validate implementation against plan, verify success criteria, identify issues
- commit: Create git commits with user approval and no Claude attribution
- oneshot_merge: Commit changes, create PR, and merge into main in one shot
- improve_plan: Adversarial review of implementation plans — find weaknesses, kill code smells, improve DX
- research_codebase: Document codebase as-is with thoughts directory for historical context
- skill-creator:skill-creator: Create new skills, modify and improve existing skills, and measure skill performance. Use when users want to create a skill from scratch, update or optimize an existing skill, run evals to test a skill, benchmark skill performance with variance analysis, or optimize a skill's description for better triggering accuracy.
- init: Initialize a new CLAUDE.md file with codebase documentation
- review: Review a pull request
- security-review: Complete a security review of the pending changes on the current branch

content.content:
1	# yaml-language-server: $schema=https://coderabbit.ai/integrations/schema.v2.json
2	language: "en-US"
3	early_access: false
4	
5	tone_instructions: "Be direct, concise, no pleasantries. Categorize: [blocker] must fix, [warning] should fix with trade-offs, [nit] optional. Cite files or CLAUDE.md sections. Explain WHY changes matter, don't restate what changed."
6	
7	reviews:
8	  profile: "chill"
9	  request_changes_workflow: false
10	
11	  # PR summary features
12	  high_level_summary: true
13	  review_status: true
14	  collapse_walkthrough: true
15	  poem: false
16	  sequence_diagrams: true
17	
18	  # Link tracking
19	  assess_linked_issues: true
20	
21	  # Auto-review triggers — runs on non-draft PRs targeting main/develop unless title opts out.
22	  # Incremental reviews run automatically on each push so pre-merge checks stay fresh.
23	  auto_review:
24	    enabled: true
25	    drafts: false
26	    auto_incremental_review: true
27	    ignore_title_keywords:
28	      - "WIP"
29	      - "DRAFT"
30	      - "DO NOT MERGE"
31	      - "[skip-review]"
32	    base_branches:
33	      - "main"
34	      - "develop"
35	
36	  # ── Path filters ──
37	  # Exclude generated/vendored/binary files from review
38	  path_filters:
39	    - "!**/dist/**"
40	    - "!**/build/**"
41	    - "!**/node_modules/**"
42	    - "!**/.next/**"
43	    - "!**/.vercel/**"
44	    - "!**/pnpm-lock.yaml"
45	    - "!**/*.lock"
46	    - "!**/drizzle/**/meta/**"
47	    - "!db/**/migrations/**"
48	    - "!**/__generated__/**"
49	    - "!**/generated/**"
50	    - "!**/*.snap"
51	    - "!**/*.min.js"
52	    - "!**/*.min.css"
53	    - "!**/*.d.ts"
54	    - "!**/coverage/**"
55	    - "!CHANGELOG.md"
56	    - "!**/CHANGELOG.md"
57	    # Non-code directories
58	    - "!thoughts/**"
59	    - "!scripts/**"
60	    - "!docs/**"
61	    - "!examples/**"
62	    - "!tmp/**"
63	    - "!worktrees/**"
64	    - "!.claude/**"
65	    - "!.agents/**"
66	
67	  # ── Path-specific review instructions ──
68	  path_instructions:
69	    - path: "**/*"
70	      instructions: |
71	        This is a pnpm monorepo (Turborepo). Key rules:
72	        - Never import third-party SDKs directly — use @vendor/* abstractions
73	        - Internal packages use workspace:* protocol; shared externals use catalog:
74	        - TypeScript strict mode is enforced everywhere
75	        - Flag any use of `any` type without a justification comment
76	
77	    - path: "apps/app/**"
78	      instructions: |
79	        Next.js 15 App Router (main app, port 4107). Review for:
80	        - Server/client component boundaries — 'use client' only when DOM APIs or hooks require it
81	        - tRPC: prefetch() must run BEFORE <HydrateClient> to avoid UNAUTHORIZED errors
82	        - Auth routes (/sign-in, /sign-up, /early-access) are served directly by this app
83	        - Check new route directories for missing error.tsx and loading.tsx
84	
85	    - path: "apps/platform/**"
86	      instructions: |
87	        Standalone Next.js + Inngest service at platform.lightfast.ai (port 4112). Review for:
88	        - OAuth tokens must go through vault abstraction — never stored raw
89	        - Inngest workflows must have idempotency keys and proper error handling
90	        - Webhook ingestion endpoints must validate provider signatures
91	
92	    - path: "apps/www/**"
93	      instructions: |
94	        Marketing + docs site using Fumadocs MDX. Review for:
95	        - Docs served via microfrontends at /docs routes
96	        - Check all external links for correctness (domain: lightfast.ai)
97	
98	    - path: "api/**"
99	      instructions: |
100	        tRPC API layer. Review for:
101	        - Auth boundaries: userRouter (no org), orgRouter (org required)
102	        - All procedures must use correct guard (userProcedure vs orgProcedure)
103	        - Inngest workflows in api/app/src/inngest/workflow/ and api/platform/src/inngest/
104	        - Inngest functions need: idempotency key, proper step boundaries, timeout handling
105	
106	    - path: "vendor/**"
107	      instructions: |
108	        Vendor abstraction packages — standalone re-exports of third-party SDKs. Review for:
109	        - Must be thin wrappers, not business logic
110	        - Consumers should never need to import the underlying SDK directly
111	        - Env vars validated at package level via env.ts
112	        - Exports must be explicit (no barrel export *)
113	
114	    - path: "packages/**"
115	      instructions: |
116	        Shared internal packages (@repo/*). Review for:
117	        - Flag all breaking changes — multiple apps consume these
118	        - New exports must be added to the package index file
119	        - Explicit named exports (no barrel export *)
120	        - Type-only imports where applicable
121	
122	    - path: "db/**"
123	      instructions: |
124	        Database packages using Drizzle ORM. Review for:
125	        - Never suggest hand-written SQL — migrations generated by drizzle-kit only
126	        - Schema changes must have a corresponding migration
127	        - Review index coverage on new foreign key columns
128	        - No raw SQL queries — use Drizzle query builder
129	
130	    - path: "core/ai-sdk/**"
131	      instructions: |
132	        Core AI SDK package (@lightfastai/ai-sdk). Review for:
133	        - Public API surface stability — breaking changes need justification
134	        - Test coverage for primitives (agent, tool, server)
135	        - Proper error handling and types
136	
137	    - path: "**/*.test.ts"
138	      instructions: |
139	        Test files. Review for:
140	        - Prefer behavior-based assertions over implementation details
141	        - Auth boundary edge cases (no org, wrong org, expired token) must be covered
142	        - No flaky patterns (timeouts, race conditions)
143	
144	    - path: ".github/workflows/**"
145	      instructions: |
146	        CI/CD workflows. Review for:
147	        - All action versions must be pinned to a SHA, not a mutable tag
148	        - Every job needs timeout-minutes to prevent runaway CI
149	        - Secrets should use GitHub Environments where possible
150	
151	  # ── Pre-merge checks ──
152	  pre_merge_checks:
153	    title:
154	      mode: "warning"
155	      requirements: "PR title should follow conventional commits format (feat:, fix:, chore:, etc.) and be under 70 characters"
156	    description:
157	      mode: "warning"
158	    issue_assessment:
159	      mode: "warning"
160	    docstrings:
161	      mode: "warning"
162	
163	# ── Tools ──
164	tools:
165	  eslint:
166	    enabled: false
167	  biome:
168	    enabled: true
169	  ast-grep:
170	    enabled: true
171	    essential_rules: true
172	  shellcheck:
173	    enabled: true
174	  actionlint:
175	    enabled: true
176	  markdownlint:
177	    enabled: true
178	  yamllint:
179	    enabled: true
180	  github-checks:
181	    enabled: true
182	  gitleaks:
183	    enabled: true
184	
185	# ── Finishing touches — one-click agentic actions in PR comments ──
186	finishing_touches:
187	  docstrings:
188	    enabled: true
189	  unit_tests:
190	    enabled: true
191	  simplify:
192	    enabled: true
193	
194	chat:
195	  auto_reply: true
196	
197	knowledge_base:
198	  opt_out: false
199	  web_search:
200	    enabled: true
201	  code_guidelines:
202	    enabled: true
203	  learnings:
204	    scope: "auto"
205	  issues:
206	    scope: "auto"
207	  pull_requests:
208	    scope: "auto"
209

file.content:
# yaml-language-server: $schema=https://coderabbit.ai/integrations/schema.v2.json
language: "en-US"
early_access: false

tone_instructions: "Be direct, concise, no pleasantries. Categorize: [blocker] must fix, [warning] should fix with trade-offs, [nit] optional. Cite files or CLAUDE.md sections. Explain WHY changes matter, don't restate what changed."

reviews:
  profile: "chill"
  request_changes_workflow: false

  # PR summary features
  high_level_summary: true
  review_status: true
  collapse_walkthrough: true
  poem: false
  sequence_diagrams: true

  # Link tracking
  assess_linked_issues: true

  # Auto-review triggers — runs on non-draft PRs targeting main/develop unless title opts out.
  # Incremental reviews run automatically on each push so pre-merge checks stay fresh.
  auto_review:
    enabled: true
    drafts: false
    auto_incremental_review: true
    ignore_title_keywords:
      - "WIP"
      - "DRAFT"
      - "DO NOT MERGE"
      - "[skip-review]"
    base_branches:
      - "main"
      - "develop"

  # ── Path filters ──
  # Exclude generated/vendored/binary files from review
  path_filters:
    - "!**/dist/**"
    - "!**/build/**"
    - "!**/node_modules/**"
    - "!**/.next/**"
    - "!**/.vercel/**"
    - "!**/pnpm-lock.yaml"
    - "!**/*.lock"
    - "!**/drizzle/**/meta/**"
    - "!db/**/migrations/**"
    - "!**/__generated__/**"
    - "!**/generated/**"
    - "!**/*.snap"
    - "!**/*.min.js"
    - "!**/*.min.css"
    - "!**/*.d.ts"
    - "!**/coverage/**"
    - "!CHANGELOG.md"
    - "!**/CHANGELOG.md"
    # Non-code directories
    - "!thoughts/**"
    - "!scripts/**"
    - "!docs/**"
    - "!examples/**"
    - "!tmp/**"
    - "!worktrees/**"
    - "!.claude/**"
    - "!.agents/**"

  # ── Path-specific review instructions ──
  path_instructions:
    - path: "**/*"
      instructions: |
        This is a pnpm monorepo (Turborepo). Key rules:
        - Never import third-party SDKs directly — use @vendor/* abstractions
        - Internal packages use workspace:* protocol; shared externals use catalog:
        - TypeScript strict mode is enforced everywhere
        - Flag any use of `any` type without a justification comment

    - path: "apps/app/**"
      instructions: |
        Next.js 15 App Router (main app, port 4107). Review for:
        - Server/client component boundaries — 'use client' only when DOM APIs or hooks require it
        - tRPC: prefetch() must run BEFORE <HydrateClient> to avoid UNAUTHORIZED errors
        - Auth routes (/sign-in, /sign-up, /early-access) are served directly by this app
        - Check new route directories for missing error.tsx and loading.tsx

    - path: "apps/platform/**"
      instructions: |
        Standalone Next.js + Inngest service at platform.lightfast.ai (port 4112). Review for:
        - OAuth tokens must go through vault abstraction — never stored raw
        - Inngest workflows must have idempotency keys and proper error handling
        - Webhook ingestion endpoints must validate provider signatures

    - path: "apps/www/**"
      instructions: |
        Marketing + docs site using Fumadocs MDX. Review for:
        - Docs served via microfrontends at /docs routes
        - Check all external links for correctness (domain: lightfast.ai)

    - path: "api/**"
      instructions: |
        tRPC API layer. Review for:
        - Auth boundaries: userRouter (no org), orgRouter (org required)
        - All procedures must use correct guard (userProcedure vs orgProcedure)
        - Inngest workflows in api/app/src/inngest/workflow/ and api/platform/src/inngest/
        - Inngest functions need: idempotency key, proper step boundaries, timeout handling

    - path: "vendor/**"
      instructions: |
        Vendor abstraction packages — standalone re-exports of third-party SDKs. Review for:
        - Must be thin wrappers, not business logic
        - Consumers should never need to import the underlying SDK directly
        - Env vars validated at package level via env.ts
        - Exports must be explicit (no barrel export *)

    - path: "packages/**"
      instructions: |
        Shared internal packages (@repo/*). Review for:
        - Flag all breaking changes — multiple apps consume these
        - New exports must be added to the package index file
        - Explicit named exports (no barrel export *)
        - Type-only imports where applicable

    - path: "db/**"
      instructions: |
        Database packages using Drizzle ORM. Review for:
        - Never suggest hand-written SQL — migrations generated by drizzle-kit only
        - Schema changes must have a corresponding migration
        - Review index coverage on new foreign key columns
        - No raw SQL queries — use Drizzle query builder

    - path: "core/ai-sdk/**"
      instructions: |
        Core AI SDK package (@lightfastai/ai-sdk). Review for:
        - Public API surface stability — breaking changes need justification
        - Test coverage for primitives (agent, tool, server)
        - Proper error handling and types

    - path: "**/*.test.ts"
      instructions: |
        Test files. Review for:
        - Prefer behavior-based assertions over implementation details
        - Auth boundary edge cases (no org, wrong org, expired token) must be covered
        - No flaky patterns (timeouts, race conditions)

    - path: ".github/workflows/**"
      instructions: |
        CI/CD workflows. Review for:
        - All action versions must be pinned to a SHA, not a mutable tag
        - Every job needs timeout-minutes to prevent runaway CI
        - Secrets should use GitHub Environments where possible

  # ── Pre-merge checks ──
  pre_merge_checks:
    title:
      mode: "warning"
      requirements: "PR title should follow conventional commits format (feat:, fix:, chore:, etc.) and be under 70 characters"
    description:
      mode: "warning"
    issue_assessment:
      mode: "warning"
    docstrings:
      mode: "warning"

# ── Tools ──
tools:
  eslint:
    enabled: false
  biome:
    enabled: true
  ast-grep:
    enabled: true
    essential_rules: true
  shellcheck:
    enabled: true
  actionlint:
    enabled: true
  markdownlint:
    enabled: true
  yamllint:
    enabled: true
  github-checks:
    enabled: true
  gitleaks:
    enabled: true

# ── Finishing touches — one-click agentic actions in PR comments ──
finishing_touches:
  docstrings:
    enabled: true
  unit_tests:
    enabled: true
  simplify:
    enabled: true

chat:
  auto_reply: true

knowledge_base:
  opt_out: false
  web_search:
    enabled: true
  code_guidelines:
    enabled: true
  learnings:
    scope: "auto"
  issues:
    scope: "auto"
  pull_requests:
    scope: "auto"

lastPrompt: Validation error: Unrecognized key(s) in object: 'tools', 'finishing_touches' in .coderabbit/yaml  coderabbit.yaml has unrecognized properties CodeRabbit is using all valid settings from your configur…

content.text: Let me verify where these keys actually belong in the CodeRabbit v2 schema.

input.prompt: Where do the keys `tools` and `finishing_touches` belong in the schema? Are they top-level properties or are they nested under `reviews`? Show the exact path and sibling keys.

content.content:
# Schema Path Analysis

Both `tools` and `finishing_touches` are **nested under `reviews`**, not top-level properties.

## Exact Paths

**`tools`:**
```
schema.properties.reviews.properties.tools
```

**`finishing_touches`:**
```
schema.properties.reviews.properties.finishing_touches
```

## Sibling Keys in `reviews`

The keys at the same nesting level include:
- `profile`
- `request_changes_workflow`
- `high_level_summary`
- `high_level_summary_instructions`
- `auto_title_placeholder`
- `review_status`
- `path_filters`
- `path_instructions`
- `auto_review`
- `pre_merge_checks`
- `tools` ← here
- `finishing_touches` ← here

Both appear in the latter portion of the `reviews` object's properties listing.

toolUseResult.originalFile:
# yaml-language-server: $schema=https://coderabbit.ai/integrations/schema.v2.json
language: "en-US"
early_access: false

tone_instructions: "Be direct, concise, no pleasantries. Categorize: [blocker] must fix, [warning] should fix with trade-offs, [nit] optional. Cite files or CLAUDE.md sections. Explain WHY changes matter, don't restate what changed."

reviews:
  profile: "chill"
  request_changes_workflow: false

  # PR summary features
  high_level_summary: true
  review_status: true
  collapse_walkthrough: true
  poem: false
  sequence_diagrams: true

  # Link tracking
  assess_linked_issues: true

  # Auto-review triggers — runs on non-draft PRs targeting main/develop unless title opts out.
  # Incremental reviews run automatically on each push so pre-merge checks stay fresh.
  auto_review:
    enabled: true
    drafts: false
    auto_incremental_review: true
    ignore_title_keywords:
      - "WIP"
      - "DRAFT"
      - "DO NOT MERGE"
      - "[skip-review]"
    base_branches:
      - "main"
      - "develop"

  # ── Path filters ──
  # Exclude generated/vendored/binary files from review
  path_filters:
    - "!**/dist/**"
    - "!**/build/**"
    - "!**/node_modules/**"
    - "!**/.next/**"
    - "!**/.vercel/**"
    - "!**/pnpm-lock.yaml"
    - "!**/*.lock"
    - "!**/drizzle/**/meta/**"
    - "!db/**/migrations/**"
    - "!**/__generated__/**"
    - "!**/generated/**"
    - "!**/*.snap"
    - "!**/*.min.js"
    - "!**/*.min.css"
    - "!**/*.d.ts"
    - "!**/coverage/**"
    - "!CHANGELOG.md"
    - "!**/CHANGELOG.md"
    # Non-code directories
    - "!thoughts/**"
    - "!scripts/**"
    - "!docs/**"
    - "!examples/**"
    - "!tmp/**"
    - "!worktrees/**"
    - "!.claude/**"
    - "!.agents/**"

  # ── Path-specific review instructions ──
  path_instructions:
    - path: "**/*"
      instructions: |
        This is a pnpm monorepo (Turborepo). Key rules:
        - Never import third-party SDKs directly — use @vendor/* abstractions
        - Internal packages use workspace:* protocol; shared externals use catalog:
        - TypeScript strict mode is enforced everywhere
        - Flag any use of `any` type without a justification comment

    - path: "apps/app/**"
      instructions: |
        Next.js 15 App Router (main app, port 4107). Review for:
        - Server/client component boundaries — 'use client' only when DOM APIs or hooks require it
        - tRPC: prefetch() must run BEFORE <HydrateClient> to avoid UNAUTHORIZED errors
        - Auth routes (/sign-in, /sign-up, /early-access) are served directly by this app
        - Check new route directories for missing error.tsx and loading.tsx

    - path: "apps/platform/**"
      instructions: |
        Standalone Next.js + Inngest service at platform.lightfast.ai (port 4112). Review for:
        - OAuth tokens must go through vault abstraction — never stored raw
        - Inngest workflows must have idempotency keys and proper error handling
        - Webhook ingestion endpoints must validate provider signatures

    - path: "apps/www/**"
      instructions: |
        Marketing + docs site using Fumadocs MDX. Review for:
        - Docs served via microfrontends at /docs routes
        - Check all external links for correctness (domain: lightfast.ai)

    - path: "api/**"
      instructions: |
        tRPC API layer. Review for:
        - Auth boundaries: userRouter (no org), orgRouter (org required)
        - All procedures must use correct guard (userProcedure vs orgProcedure)
        - Inngest workflows in api/app/src/inngest/workflow/ and api/platform/src/inngest/
        - Inngest functions need: idempotency key, proper step boundaries, timeout handling

    - path: "vendor/**"
      instructions: |
        Vendor abstraction packages — standalone re-exports of third-party SDKs. Review for:
        - Must be thin wrappers, not business logic
        - Consumers should never need to import the underlying SDK directly
        - Env vars validated at package level via env.ts
        - Exports must be explicit (no barrel export *)

    - path: "packages/**"
      instructions: |
        Shared internal packages (@repo/*). Review for:
        - Flag all breaking changes — multiple apps consume these
        - New exports must be added to the package index file
        - Explicit named exports (no barrel export *)
        - Type-only imports where applicable

    - path: "db/**"
      instructions: |
        Database packages using Drizzle ORM. Review for:
        - Never suggest hand-written SQL — migrations generated by drizzle-kit only
        - Schema changes must have a corresponding migration
        - Review index coverage on new foreign key columns
        - No raw SQL queries — use Drizzle query builder

    - path: "core/ai-sdk/**"
      instructions: |
        Core AI SDK package (@lightfastai/ai-sdk). Review for:
        - Public API surface stability — breaking changes need justification
        - Test coverage for primitives (agent, tool, server)
        - Proper error handling and types

    - path: "**/*.test.ts"
      instructions: |
        Test files. Review for:
        - Prefer behavior-based assertions over implementation details
        - Auth boundary edge cases (no org, wrong org, expired token) must be covered
        - No flaky patterns (timeouts, race conditions)

    - path: ".github/workflows/**"
      instructions: |
        CI/CD workflows. Review for:
        - All action versions must be pinned to a SHA, not a mutable tag
        - Every job needs timeout-minutes to prevent runaway CI
        - Secrets should use GitHub Environments where possible

  # ── Pre-merge checks ──
  pre_merge_checks:
    title:
      mode: "warning"
      requirements: "PR title should follow conventional commits format (feat:, fix:, chore:, etc.) and be under 70 characters"
    description:
      mode: "warning"
    issue_assessment:
      mode: "warning"
    docstrings:
      mode: "warning"

# ── Tools ──
tools:
  eslint:
    enabled: false
  biome:
    enabled: true
  ast-grep:
    enabled: true
    essential_rules: true
  shellcheck:
    enabled: true
  actionlint:
    enabled: true
  markdownlint:
    enabled: true
  yamllint:
    enabled: true
  github-checks:
    enabled: true
  gitleaks:
    enabled: true

# ── Finishing touches — one-click agentic actions in PR comments ──
finishing_touches:
  docstrings:
    enabled: true
  unit_tests:
    enabled: true
  simplify:
    enabled: true

chat:
  auto_reply: true

knowledge_base:
  opt_out: false
  web_search:
    enabled: true
  code_guidelines:
    enabled: true
  learnings:
    scope: "auto"
  issues:
    scope: "auto"
  pull_requests:
    scope: "auto"

+  # ── Finishing touches — one-click agentic actions in PR comments ──
